name: Yucatan Seafood Stew
recipeIngredient: 2 Pasilla Chilies, roasted and peeled with skin stripped
1 large Garlic head, roasted and chopped (or Chipotle or Jalapeno chilies)
8 Little Neck Clams, or Manilla Clams, 8 oz each
2 Lobster tails, parboiled (up to two pounds)
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat olive oil in a large saute pan and add onion. Saute until translucent.
2. Add roasted garlic (or chopped chilies) and cook for another minute.
3. Stir in bay leaves and allspice.
4. Meanwhile, heat fish or chicken stock in a saucepan.
5. Add coriander bunches to the pan and stir in clams and grilled tomatoes. Cook until almost everything is hot.
6. Stir in 2 cups of heated stock, cover, and steam for about 5 minutes, adding more stock if needed to keep ingredients covered.
7. Add angler fish, lime juice (to taste), and chilies. Adjust with more coriander or roasted garlic if desired.
8. Continue cooking until clams open, then boil vigorously for 2-3 minutes.
9. Stir in the remaining fish (tuna or cod), lobster, shrimp, oysters, and squid.
10. Cook covered for another 2-3 minutes.
11. Remove coriander bunch, serve hot, garnished with fresh chopped cilantro.
